page_title: Tokenized Money Market Funds — Digital Assets Dashboard
page_subtitle_html: Tokenized money market fund (MMF) exposure aggregated from RWA.xyz US Treasuries and Non-U.S. Government Debt. Networks and platforms are built from each fund's on-chain token deployments; Distributed Value columns are current levels.
kpi_window_note: Distributed value uses a 30-day (30D) % change vs summed token values 30 days ago. 30D net change is the dollar change in total distributed value across all funds in the same window. Fund universe: tokenized money market funds on RWA.xyz US Treasuries and Non-U.S. Government Debt pages.

bottom_cta.label: See US Treasuries on RWA.xyz (MMF listings)

key_observations_html:
• TMMFs are becoming a de facto settlement layer for institutions: several of the largest funds combine very high AUM with small holder counts—e.g. USYC (44 holders, $2.96B; ~$67M/holder), iBENJI (28 holders, $1.59B; ~$57M/holder), BUIDL (109 holders, $2.42B; ~$22M/holder)—suggesting on-chain cash and collateral rails rather than broad retail savings products. Ethereum still carries ~43% of aggregated MMF network share, alongside selective deployment on chains used for institutional workflows.
  • Early "flight to efficiency" shows up in 30D network share: gainers include Solana (+2.31 pp 30D share), Stellar (+1.99 pp 30D share); losers include BNB Chain (-5.72 pp 30D share), Arbitrum (-0.53 pp 30D share). That pattern is consistent with liquidity migrating toward higher-throughput, lower-fee chains—though Ethereum still dominates levels in most snapshots.
  • Industry headlines emphasize Chain efficiency & migration Recent coverage includes Kalshi Eyes Perpetual Futures for XRP, Solana, Dogecoin—And These Altcoins (Decrypt); IG introduces zero-commission crypto trading for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana - IFA Magazine (Google News (bitcoin ethereum markets)). Fee and throughput narratives frequently foreshadow 30D network-share migration among tokenized funds—check whether gainers in the By network table match the chains named in coverage.
  • Industry headlines emphasize Regulation & compliance Recent coverage includes Telegram's Durov Renames TON's Native Token to Gram, Price Climbs as Much as 19% (The Defiant); MiCA Review: European Commission Launches Consultation on EU Cryptoasset Regulatory Framework - fintechanddigitalassets.com (Google News (digital assets)). Cross-border compliance stories often explain why similar funds sit under different regulatory labels—jurisdiction and investor-eligibility columns on this page are the practical read-through.
  • Industry headlines emphasize Institutional settlement & cash rails Recent coverage includes NYDIG suggests $1.3B IBIT sale was whale exiting directional trade (CoinTelegraph); Bitwise completes takeover of Superstate's $259M crypto carry fund (CoinTelegraph). Settlement and cash-rail headlines align with funds that combine large AUM with small holder bases—compare with the funds table and whether issuers are positioning products as on-chain liquidity, not retail savings.

Context only—not investment advice. Observations rank fund-level and network aggregates on this page against recent tokenization and money-market headlines. Bullets are ranked from on-page data and recent industry headlines (dashboard RSS plus Google News fallback).

Key observations are AI generated and should be reviewed for accuracy.

funds_table.search_placeholder: Filter by fund name, ticker, platform, network, domicile…

networks.caption_html: Networks table sums distributed value across tokenized money market fund token deployments on each chain. 7D Δ value and market share are computed from aggregated token rows (RWA.xyz US Treasuries and Non-U.S. Government Debt fund lists).
networks.chart_note_html: The chart lists the top 12 networks by total value (labels include market share). Scroll the table for the full filtered list.
networks.section_intro_html: Networks — aggregate distributed value of tokenized money market funds by chain, summed from each fund's token deployments.

platforms.caption_html: Platforms table groups the same fund universe by asset manager (issuer). RWA Count is the number of distinct MMF products with exposure for that manager.
platforms.chart_note_html: The chart lists the top 12 platforms by total value (labels include market share). Scroll the table for the full filtered list.
platforms.section_intro_html: Platforms — issuer / asset-manager aggregates for tokenized MMFs (same measures as Networks).
